title: Flexible Functions
description: Make functions adaptable with parameters - learn to pass data into your functions.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Functions become really powerful when they can accept input. That's what parameters are for!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Like how move() can accept coordinates to move to different plots?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Coordinates are arguments passed to move's parameters. Let's create a function with parameters for plot positions.

objectives.description: Define a function with a parameter: def move_to_plot(plot_x):

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! plot_x is now a parameter—a variable that receives input when the function is called.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Inside the function, you can use the parameter like any variable. Use plot_x in a move command inside your function.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So whatever I pass to move_to_plot gets used when moving?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! The parameter acts as a placeholder for whatever value you provide.

objectives.description: Use the parameter inside your function body. Using move_right

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Great! Now your function can move to any plot position based on what you pass in.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now call your function with different arguments. Try calling move_to_plot with different x coordinates!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So I can call the same function with different values?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! That's the flexibility of parameters. One function, infinite possibilities!

objectives.description: Call your function with coordinate 3 too
Execute to see your parameterized function work

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Amazing! One function moved to different plots!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: That's the beauty of parameters—reusable code that adapts to different inputs.

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Functions can have multiple parameters! Create a function that takes both x and y coordinates to move to any plot position.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So I separate them with commas in the definition?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! And when calling, you provide values in the same order. Multiple parameters make functions very flexible!

objectives.description: Create a function with multiple parameters (2 or more)
Your function should have comma-separated parameters

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: Multiple parameters make functions so much more powerful!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Absolutely! Now your functions can accept all the information they need to do complex tasks. Next, we'll learn how functions can send data back!

rewards.description: Unlocked quest: Functions That Give Back
